Trigger_Event: Unexpected Notification from the contractor on the increase in the structural price because of alleged delay by Owner in approval of structural steel shop drawings
Conflict_Progression: It was a Financial issue triggered by subcontractor’s escalated quote on structural material to the contractor and in turned the contractor blamed this issue on the owner by cooking up unsubstantiated delay in shop drawings which had no bearing on the cobtractor’s ability to issue P.O/mill order on structural steel to the subcontractor
Additional_Comments: The contractor’s claim went into protracted litigation and was eventually settled before the trial in favor of the owner
Conflict_Severity_Label: High / Critical: Major disputes leading to arbitration, litigation, significant delays, or project suspension.

Trigger_Event: That meeting changed the tone from “payment coordination” to “performance blame”. From that point onward, discussions were no longer solution-oriented but trust-defensive, and the conflict began escalating.
Conflict_Progression: A financial issue gradually turned into a trust issue, then into a productivity issue, and finally into a project delay issue.
Hidden_Factors: What began as a solvable financial/technical issue became difficult to resolve due to human behavior, communication gaps, and loss of professional trust.
Conflict_Severity_Label: Medium: Frequent disagreements that required senior intervention; minor impact on time/cost.
text_blob: That meeting changed the tone from “payment coordination” to “performance blame”. From that point onward, discussions were no longer solution-oriented but trust-defensive, and the conflict began escalating. A financial issue gradually turned into a trust issue, then into a productivity issue, and finally into a project delay issue. What began as a solvable financial/technical issue became difficult to resolve due to human behavior, communication gaps, and loss of professional trust.
